modules.title: Introduction to optical fibers and light propagation
topics: Propagation of light in free space and in a cylindrical dielectric rod
Ray model and wave model of light propagation
Structure and wave‑guiding fundamentals of optical fibers

modules.title: Signal degradation in optical fibers
topics: Dispersion and attenuation in optical fibers

modules.title: Optical sources and detectors
topics: LEDs and laser diodes as optical sources

modules.title: Optical transceivers and fiber interconnection
topics: Digital receiver performance evaluation and BER calculation
System design, power budgeting, rise‑time budgeting

modules.title: Optical networks and amplifiers
topics: Optical amplifiers: EDFAs and Raman amplifiers

reference_books: Keiser G, Optical Fibre Communication, 5th ed. 2006, McGrawHill.
Mynbanv and Scheiner, Fibre Optic Communication Technology, 2nd ed. 2010, Pearson Education.
Djfar K Mynbaev & Scheiner, Fibre Optic Communication Technology, 5th ed. 2005, Pearson.

modules.title: Orthogonal transforms
topics: Parallel all‑pass realization of IIR transfer functions

modules.title: Multirate signal processing
topics: Multistage design of interpolators and decimators

reference_books: Stuart Russell, Peter Norvig: “Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach”, 2nd Edition, Pearson Education, 2007
Artificial Neural Networks, B. Yagna Narayana, PHI
Artificial Intelligence, 2nd Edition, E. Rich and K. Knight (TMH)
Artificial Intelligence and Expert Systems – Patterson, PHI
S. K. Mitra: “Digital Signal Processing: A Computer‑Based Approach” (McGraw‑Hill)
E. C. Ifeacthor and B. W. Jervis: “Digital Signal Processing: A Practical Approach” (Pearson)
R. Chassaing and D. Reay, Digital Signal Processing and Applications with TMS320C6713 and TMS320C6416, Wiley, 2008
J. G. Proakis and D. G. Manolakis, Digital Signal Processing

modules.title: Introduction
topics: Applications and requirements of wireless services – history, service types, performance requirements, economic and social aspects.
Technical challenges in wireless communications – multipath propagation, spectrum scarcity, limited energy, user mobility, noise and interference‑limited environments.
Propagation mechanisms – free‑space loss, reflection and transmission, diffraction, scattering by rough surfaces, and wave guiding.

modules.title: Wireless Propagation Channels
topics: Statistical description of the wireless channel – time‑invariant and time‑variant two‑path models, small‑scale fading (with/without dominant component), Doppler spectra, temporal fading dependence, and large‑scale fading.

Wideband and directional channel characteristics – delay dispersion causes, system‑theoretic description of wireless channels, WSSUS model, condensed parameters, ultra‑wideband channels, and directional descriptions.

modules.title: Channel Models
topics: Channel models – narrowband, wideband and directional models; deterministic channel‑modeling methods.
Channel sounding – introduction, time‑domain measurements, frequency‑domain analysis, modified measurement techniques, and directionally resolved measurements.
Antennas – fundamentals, antennas for mobile stations, and antennas for base stations.

modules.title: Transceivers and Signal Processing
topics: Structure of a wireless communication link – transceiver block diagram and simplified models.
Error probability analysis in additive white Gaussian noise (\(\text{AWGN}\)) channels, flat‑fading channels, and delay/frequency‑dispersive fading channels.

modules.title: Diversity
topics: Diversity techniques – introduction, micro‑diversity, macro‑diversity, simulcast, signal combination, error probability with diversity reception, and transmit diversity.

Equalization methods – introduction, linear equalizers, decision‑feedback equalizers, maximum‑likelihood sequence estimation (Viterbi detector), comparative analysis of equalizer structures, fractional‑spaced equalizers, and blind equalizers.

reference_books: Molisch: Wireless Communications, Wiley India.
Taub and Schilling: Principles of Communication Systems, TMH.
Haykin: Mordern Wireless Communication, Pearson Education.
Upena Dalal: Wireless Communication, Oxford University Press.
Rappaport: Wireless Communication, Pearson Education.
Price: Wireless Communication and Networks, TMH.

modules.title: Overview of 5G Broadband Wireless Communications
topics: Evaluation of mobile technologies 1G to 4G (LTE, LTE‑A, LTE‑A Pro)

modules.title: The 5G Wireless Propagation Channels
topics: Propagation scenarios and challenges in 5G modeling
Channel models for mmWave MIMO systems

modules.title: Transmission and Design Techniques for 5G
topics: Modulation techniques – OFDM, GFDM, FBMC, UFMC
Multiple access techniques – OFDMA, GFDMA, NOMA

modules.title: Device‑to‑Device (D2D) and Machine‑to‑Machine (M2M) Communications
topics: Radio resource management for mobile broadband D2D

modules.title: Millimeter‑Wave Communications
topics: Spectrum regulations and deployment scenarios for mmWave
Interference and mobility management in mmWave systems
Multi‑cell massive MIMO and pilot contamination

reference_books: Martin Sauter, “From GSM to LTE‑Advanced Pro and 5G: An Introduction to Mobile Networks and Mobile Broadband”, Wiley‑Blackwell.
Afif Osseiran, Jose F. Monserrat, Patrick Marsch, “Fundamentals of 5G Mobile Networks”, Cambridge University Press.
Athanasios G. Kanatos, Konstantina S. Nikita, Panagiotis Mathiopoulos, “New Directions in Wireless Communication Systems from Mobile to 5G”, CRC Press.
Theodore S. Rappaport, Robert W. Heath, Robert C. Danials, James N. Murdock, “Millimeter‑Wave Wireless Communications”, Prentice Hall Communications.
Jonathan Rodriguez, “Fundamentals of 5G Mobile Networks”, John Wiley & Sons.
Amitabha Ghosh and Rapeepat Ratasuk, “Essentials of LTE and LTE‑A”, Cambridge University Press.

modules.title: Review of Cellular Networks
topics: Mobile telephony, GSM, CDMA/CD and UMTS
Advancement and migrations of cellular technologies
WLAN PHY and MAC layers – IEEE 802.11 (a, b, g, ac)
WiMAX PHY and MAC layers – IEEE 802.16 (fixed and mobile)

modules.title: LTE Systems
topics: Introduction to 3GPP, LTE and LTE‑A standards
E‑UTRAN architecture, mobility and resource management

modules.title: Wireless Sensor Networks
topics: Introduction to wireless sensor networks (WSN) and architecture
Coverage planning and node placement strategies
Applications of WSNs and mobile WSN scenarios
Technologies for sensor nodes and network platforms
Routing and transport protocols for WSNs

modules.title: Wireless Routing Protocols
topics: Medium access problems in wireless networks
Mobile network layer and Mobile IP concepts
Introduction to IPv4 and IPv6 addressing
Data forwarding procedures in Mobile IP (IPv4 & IPv6)
Mobile transport layer protocols: Traditional TCP, Mobile TCP, Indirect TCP, Reno, New‑Reno, Tahoe, Vegas, UDP

modules.title: Internet of Things (IoT) and GPS Systems
topics: IoT architecture, design principles and required capabilities
IoT devices and gateways; case studies (body‑area sensor networks, smart homes, smart vehicles, smart manufacturing)
Emerging IoT standards and protocols (IEEE 802.15 WPAN, Bluetooth, ZigBee, UWB, RFID)
IoT protocol stack and interfacing between 802.11 and Bluetooth

reference_books: Kazem Sohraby, Daniel Minoli and Taieb Znati – "Wireless Sensor Networks: Technology, Protocols and Applications" – Wiley

Jan Holler, Vlasios Tsiatsis, Catherine Mulligan, Stefan Aves, Stamatis Karnouskos, David Boyle – "From Machine-to-Machine to the Internet of Things: Introduction to a New Age of Intelligence" – Academic Press, 1st Edition, 2014
Ramji Prasad – "OFDM for Wireless Communication"
Steve Rackley – "Wireless Networking Technology"

modules.title: Digital Image Processing (DIP)
topics: Introduction to digital image processing, including examples of application fields and the fundamental steps of DIP
Components of an image processing system
Digital image fundamentals: elements of visual perception, image sensing and acquisition, image sampling and quantization, basic relationships between pixels

modules.title: Image Transforms
topics: Two‑dimensional impulse and its shifting properties
$2D$ Discrete Fourier Transform ($\text{DFT}$) and its properties
Other transforms and their properties: Cosine transform, Sine transform, Walsh transform, Hadamard transform, Haar transform, Slant transform, Karhunen‑Lo\`eve (KL) transform

modules.title: Image Enhancement
topics: Spatial‑domain methods: basic intensity transformation functions, fundamentals of spatial filtering, smoothing spatial filters (linear and non‑linear), sharpening spatial filters (unsharp masking and high‑boost filtering), combined spatial enhancement techniques

Frequency‑domain methods: fundamentals of filtering in the frequency domain, image smoothing filters (Butterworth and Gaussian low‑pass filters), image sharpening filters (Butterworth and Gaussian high‑pass filters), selective frequency filtering

modules.title: Image Restoration
topics: Noise models and restoration by spatial filtering
Linear shift‑invariant degradations and estimation of the degradation function
Inverse filtering, Wiener filtering, and image reconstruction from projections

modules.title: Image Compression
topics: Fundamentals of data compression: basic compression methods such as Huffman coding, Golomb coding, LZW coding, Run‑Length coding, and symbol‑based coding
Digital image watermarking and representation/description techniques, including the Minimum Perimeter Polygons (MPP) algorithm

reference_books: Gonzalez and Woods: Digital Image Processing, Pearson Education.
Anil Jain: Fundamentals of Digital Image Processing, PHI Learning.
Annadurai: Fundamentals of Digital Image Processing, Pearson Education.
Sonka, Hlavac and Boyle: Digital Image Processing and Computer Vision, Cengage Learning.
Chanda and Majumder: Digital Image Processing and Analysis, PHI Learning.
Jayaraman, Esakkirajan and Veerakumar: Digital Image Processing, TMH.
William K. Pratt, Digital Image Processing, Wiley India.

modules.title: Basic Concepts of Speech Processing
topics: Articulatory Phonetics – Production and Classification of Speech Sounds
Acoustic Phonetics – acoustics of speech production
Review of Digital Signal Processing concepts

modules.title: Speech Analysis
topics: Speech distortion measures – mathematical and perceptual
Spectral Distortion using a Warped Frequency Scale

modules.title: Speech Recognition
topics: Architecture of large‑vocabulary continuous speech recognition system

modules.title: Speech Synthesis
topics: Intelligibility and naturalness – role of prosody

reference_books: Lawrence Rabiner and Biing‑Hwang Juang, “Fundamentals of Speech Recognition”, Pearson Education, 2003.
Daniel Jurafsky and James H. Martin, “Speech and Language Processing – An Introduction to Natural Language Processing, Computational Linguistics, and Speech Recognition”, Pearson Education.
Steven W. Smith, “The Scientist and Engineer’s Guide to Digital Signal Processing”, California Technical Publishing.
Thomas F. Quatieri, “Discrete‑Time Speech Signal Processing – Principles and Practice”, Pearson Education.
Claudio Becchetti and Lucio Prina Ricotti, “Speech Recognition”, John Wiley and Sons, 1999.
Ben Gold and Nelson Morgan, “Speech and Audio Signal Processing”, Wiley‑India Edition, 2006.
Frederick Jelinek, “Statistical Methods of Speech Recognition”, MIT Press.

Time Division Multiplexing of 2 Bandlimited Signals
